# neon_anonymized_branch (Resource)

Manages a Neon anonymized branch with masking rules.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "neon_anonymized_branch" "example" {
  project_id         = neon_project.example.id
  name               = "anonymized-branch"
  start_anonymization = true

  masking_rules {
    database_name    = "mydb"
    schema_name      = "public"
    table_name       = "users"
    column_name      = "email"
    masking_function = "anon.fake_email()"
  }

  masking_rules {
    database_name = "mydb"
    schema_name   = "public"
    table_name    = "users"
    column_name   = "name"
    masking_value = "'REDACTED'"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `project_id` (String) The project ID.

### Optional

- `masking_rules` (Block List) List of masking rules to apply to the branch. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--masking_rules))
- `name` (String) The branch name.
- `parent_id` (String) The parent branch ID. If omitted, the branch will be created from the project's default branch.
- `start_anonymization` (Boolean) If true, automatically start anonymization. Changing from true to false requires resource replacement.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) The creation timestamp.
- `id` (String) The branch ID.
- `state` (String) The current state of the anonymized branch (e.g., created, initialized, anonymizing, anonymized, error).
- `updated_at` (String) The last update timestamp.

<a id="nestedblock--masking_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `masking_rules`

Required:

- `column_name` (String) The name of the column to be masked.
- `database_name` (String) The name of the database containing the table to be masked.
- `schema_name` (String) The name of the schema containing the table to be masked.
- `table_name` (String) The name of the table containing the column to be masked.

Optional:

- `masking_function` (String) The PostgreSQL Anonymizer masking function to apply (e.g., 'anon.random_string(10)', 'anon.fake_email()').
- `masking_value` (String) A literal value to set on the column when masking.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import neon_anonymized_branch.example <project_id>/<branch_id>
```
